article_title: Mars Express Images Shalbatana Vallis, a 1,300 km Equatorial Flood Channel That Burst From Mars 3.5 Billion Years Ago
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean ESA-primary briefing. Every quantitative claim (1,300 km / length-of-Italy, 10 km wide, 500 m deep, 3.5 billion years) verified verbatim across snapshots. Both ESA direct quotes ('huge quantities of groundwater rose up to Mars's surface', 'catastrophic floodwaters cut into the rock and surged downhill, rapidly creating the winding, waterworn valleys we see here', the chaotic-terrain water-ice-melting mechanism, 'volcanic ash blown about by martian winds') verified character-for-character in source-0. Chryse Planitia ancient-ocean hypothesis correctly attributed with 'some scientists' / 'some researchers' hedging — Space.com and ScienceDaily both use exactly that framing.

source-0.status: ESA primary release. Verifies: Shalbatana Vallis name, 3.5 billion years ago, 10 km wide / 500 m deep dimensions, HRSC + Mars Express, all direct quotes (groundwater, floodwaters, chaotic terrain mechanism, volcanic ash framing), Chryse Planitia terminus.

source-1.status: Space.com. Verifies: 1,300 km 'length of Italy' framing, channel dimensions (10 km / 500 m), Chryse Planitia ancient-ocean hypothesis attributed to 'some researchers'.

source-2.status: ScienceDaily. Verifies: 'leading some scientists to suggest that the area may once have contained a large ocean' framing, outflow-channels-end-at-Chryse-Planitia geography, 10 km / 500 m dimensions.

source-3.status: Tech Times. Verifies: volcanic ash 'redistributed by powerful Martian winds over long periods of time' framing. Allowlist warning only — content fully consistent with the ESA primary.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well structured (Overview / What We Know / Chryse Planitia Connection / What We Don't Know / Why It Matters). Neutral, scientifically cautious tone. Length appropriate for Briefing (~613 words). Honest hedging on the ancient-ocean hypothesis — explicitly notes it's not consensus.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of this Mars Express HRSC scene. No archive collision.
recommendations: Add techtimes.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as a follow-up — Tech Times is a recurring science/tech news outlet whose content here is fully consistent with the ESA primary.

content_preview.title: Mars Express Images Shalbatana Vallis, a 1,300 km Equatorial Flood Channel That Burst From Mars 3.5 Billion Years Ago
content_preview.summary: ESA's Mars Express has released a new HRSC image of Shalbatana Vallis, a 1,300 km equatorial channel that was carved when groundwater erupted onto the Martian surface roughly 3.5 billion years ago.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Space Agency has released a new image of Shalbatana Vallis, a long winding valley near Mars's equator that was carved when groundwater burst onto the surface roughly 3.5 billion years ago, [according to ESA](https://www.esa.int/Science_Exploration/Space_Science/Mars_Express/Waterworn_chaos_on_Mars). The view, captured by the Mars Express orbiter's High Resolution Stereo Camera (HRSC), is the latest in a series of Mars Express scenes that document how catastrophic floods...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
